summary.summary: Imported via zip fallback, file count: 586

core_modules.description: Core HTTP/query abstraction layer used by most astroquery service clients; best central integration point for cross-cutting MCP wrapping (timeouts, tracing, retries, auth flow hooks).
Shared TAP protocol client implementation reused by multiple providers (Gaia/ESA/etc.); ideal for protocol-level MCP tools (job launch, async polling, table metadata).
High-usage object/name resolution and catalog query client; good candidate for first MCP domain toolset.
Large catalog discovery/query interface; pairs naturally with SIMBAD for broad astronomy assistant workflows.
Mission-specific TAP-heavy client; useful for advanced ADQL/async job MCP tools after base TAP integration.
Observation/product search and download workflows; suitable for MCP resource browsing + retrieval tools.
Widely used survey query client with imaging/spectra access patterns valuable for end-user MCP queries.
Solar-system ephemerides vectors/elements service; high-value specialized MCP astronomy tool.

mcp_plugin_strategy.recommended_pattern: thin adapter over existing class-based clients
why: Repository exposes rich importable Python APIs but no CLI entry points.
Service modules are already decoupled by provider, enabling selective tool exposure.
Common infrastructure (query.py + TAP stack) enables shared MCP middleware with minimal code changes.

phased_plan.notes: Expose read/query-only MCP tools first; avoid login-required flows initially.
Add ADQL async job lifecycle tools (submit/status/fetch/cancel) with strong timeout/cost guards.
Add domain-specialized endpoints and normalized result formatting.

minimal_intrusion_suggestions: Do not fork provider logic; wrap imported classes in an external MCP server package.
Implement a small compatibility layer that normalizes astropy Table/object outputs to JSON-safe schemas.
Use composition, not inheritance, for wrappers to avoid coupling to astroquery internals.
Centralize network controls (timeout, max rows, async polling interval) in adapter config, not upstream code.
Treat authenticated services as opt-in tools gated by environment/config secrets.
Reuse existing astroquery caching behavior; add MCP-level cache keying only for deterministic read tools.
Map astroquery exceptions to stable MCP error codes without modifying astroquery source.
